This report does not update earnings guidance and does not relate to production capacity, customer churn, or other KPIs; it reflects a contained operational matter already addressed and publicly reported.

Lianhai Smart Logistics Co., Ltd. (Unified Social Credit Code: 9132TEST0000X9A12Q; stock short name “Lianhai Zhi Yun”, code DHE:02188 on the Donghai Stock Exchange) — Cybersecurity Incident Summary (stale disclosure referenced).
On 2025-03-02 at 07:46 CST, our SOC detected abnormal credential attempts against a non-production log aggregation endpoint; the system auto-isolated the endpoint by 07:53 CST and no service outage occurred.
Affected data comprised hashed device identifiers and service telemetry; no customer account credentials, PRC resident ID numbers (only masked placeholders like 99************123X in test logs), payment data, or precise location traces were involved.
Based on PIPL and internal classification, no sensitive personal information of natural persons was processed, and no data of minors under 14 years of age was identified.
No cross-border transfers occurred; a small set of anonymized indicators (hashes only) was later shared with our third-party threat intel vendor for malware signature comparison under a standard data entrustment addendum recorded with the Yongzhou Cyberspace Administration on 2024-10-15.
This incident and our remedial steps were publicly disclosed via “临时公告” on the Donghai Stock Exchange information disclosure platform at 2025-03-02 09:30 CST, with the designated media being Donghai Securities Daily; a general press note was posted the same morning.
Issuer-relative materiality was evaluated per the Exchange Listing Rules and the Information Disclosure Management Measures, and the event was determined not to constitute a “重大事项”; no trading suspension or correction announcement was required.
Prior to the 09:30 public posting, at 08:12 CST an operations manager posted a brief incident note to a restricted supplier portal (access controlled to 12 suppliers) during the standard pre-annual report blackout window (2025-02-28 to 2025-03-15), creating a potential selective disclosure concern under GSRC guidance; this was logged and addressed.
The supplier note contained only high-level operational status (“logs isolated; no client impact”) and no financial forecasts or undisclosed performance indicators; the content was superseded by the formal public announcement the same morning.
Remediation included credential resets, IP blocklists, and vendor review; our threat intel vendor Guangyue CloudSec Technology Co., Ltd. (USCC: 9144FAKE0000M2B56Y) confirmed no onward sharing of any indicators beyond contractual scope.
For compliance, “内幕信息知情人登记” records were updated, routine pre-clearance for external communications was reiterated to all departments, and quarterly training on “选择性信息披露” controls has been scheduled for Q2 (attendance tracked by HR workflows).
After the 09:30 disclosure, the information is considered publicly known; employees were reminded that routine references to the already-posted announcement in customer FAQs do not constitute dissemination of insider information.
This report does not update earnings guidance and does not relate to production capacity, customer churn, or other KPIs; it reflects a contained operational matter already addressed and publicly reported.
